methods.description: Process for preparing seawater as a medium for culturing marine diatom C. simplex.

steps.description: The seawater was collected at 10 km away from shore of the Parangipettai coast.
The seawater was kept in a dark room for three months.
steps.searchable_summary: store seawater in dark room for aging

steps.description: The aged seawater was filtered through 0.2 µ membrane filter.
steps.searchable_summary: filter aged seawater using 0.2 µ membrane filter

steps.description: The filtered seawater was amended with f/2 nutrients, sterilized by autoclaving at 121°C for 15 minutes and used as medium.
steps.searchable_summary: amend filtered seawater with nutrients and sterilize

methods.description: Measurement of various physico-chemical parameters of industrial effluent.

steps.description: The industrial effluent was collected from discharging site of Cuddalore industrial area, Tamil Nadu.
steps.searchable_summary: collect industrial effluent from specified site

steps.description: Temperature was determined by Celsius thermometer.
pH by Electronic pH meter (ELICO, Model LI 120).
steps.searchable_summary: measure pH using electronic pH meter

steps.description: Turbidity by Digital Turbidity meter (ELICO, Model 331).

steps.searchable_summary: measure dissolved oxygen using Winkler's method

steps.description: Alkalinity, Ammonia, Nitrite, Nitrate, Total Phosphate, Inorganic Phosphate, Reactive Silicate, Calcium and Magnesium by the method of Strickland and Parsons, (1972).
steps.searchable_summary: measure various chemical parameters using Strickland and Parsons method

methods.method_name: Experimental Design and Growth Rate Measurement
methods.description: Experimental setup to estimate the effect of industrial effluent on marine diatom C. simplex and measure growth rate.

steps.description: A subculture was prepared in 5 litre culture flask with three litres of f/2 media and it was allowed to reach exponential phase for 4 days.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare subculture in flask and grow to exponential phase

steps.description: Experiments were conducted in duplicate to estimate the effect of industrial effluent by exposing the marine diatom, C. simplex with different concentrations of the industrial effluent for 96 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct experiments with varying effluent concentrations

steps.description: The cell growth was monitored at the beginning and end of the experiment by measuring cell numbers by manual counting under the binocular light microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: measure cell growth by manual counting

steps.description: Growth rates were calculated as µ.day −1 according to the formula where N0 and N1 represent cell density at the start and the end of the growth period, and t is the time between measurements (in days).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate growth rates using cell density data
